comment_text.sn4.24:1.2: According to the commentarial reckoning, this was in the fifth week after Awakening.
comment_text.sn4.24:1.3: This series of verses is a cleverly structured argument where Māra tries one method after another, always seeking to find something in the Buddha’s words or deeds that make him vulnerable. | Siddhattha had practiced for six years and had now only just realized Awakening, so either the number seven has been rounded up, or else Māra began his work before Siddhattha left home.
comment_text.sn4.24:2.1: Māra begins the contest of wits by insinuating that something must have gone wrong for the Buddha to end up where he is, trying to play on feelings of self-doubt.
comment_text.sn4.24:3.1: The Buddha rejoins with a series of bold and incontrovertible claims, shutting down the idea that he is self-loathing.
comment_text.sn4.24:4.1: Māra changes tack. Having failed to insinuate self-doubt, now he tries to use the Buddha’s very confidence against him. Surely he must be conceited to make such claims.
comment_text.sn4.24:5.1: The Buddha rejects the charge of conceit, as he has merely spoken of the truth, not of what he is attached to.
comment_text.sn4.24:5.3: The Buddha goes on the attack, asserting the limits of Māra’s knowledge.
comment_text.sn4.24:6.1: Māra accepts, for rhetorical purposes, the Buddha’s claim that he has found a path Māra cannot see. Better, he argues, for the Buddha to walk it alone and not teach anyone.
comment_text.sn4.24:7.1: The Buddha rejects this final parry, asserting that he will teach anyone who asks.
comment_text.sn4.24:8.4: The crab “extends” a claw as a meditator after absorption “extends” the mind to insight (MN 4:27.1). See too the simile at SN 35.240.

root_text.sn4.24:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā uruvelāyaṁ viharati najjā nerañjarāya tīre ajapālanigrodhe.
root_text.sn4.24:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena māro pāpimā sattavassāni bhagavantaṁ anubandho hoti otārāpekkho otāraṁ alabhamāno.
root_text.sn4.24:1.4: Atha kho māro pāpimā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ gāthāya ajjhabhāsi:
root_text.sn4.24:8.1: “Seyyathāpi, bhante, gāmassa vā nigamassa vā avidūre pokkharaṇī.
root_text.sn4.24:8.3: Atha kho, bhante, sambahulā kumārakā vā kumārikāyo vā tamhā gāmā vā nigamā vā nikkhamitvā yena sā pokkharaṇī tenupasaṅkameyyuṁ; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ kakkaṭakaṁ udakā uddharitvā thale patiṭṭhapeyyuṁ.
root_text.sn4.24:8.4: Yaṁ yadeva hi so, bhante, kakkaṭako aḷaṁ abhininnāmeyya taṁ tadeva te kumārakā vā kumārikāyo vā kaṭṭhena vā kathalāya vā sañchindeyyuṁ sambhañjeyyuṁ sampalibhañjeyyuṁ.
root_text.sn4.24:8.5: Evañhi so, bhante, kakkaṭako sabbehi aḷehi sañchinnehi sambhaggehi sampalibhaggehi abhabbo taṁ pokkharaṇiṁ otarituṁ.
root_text.sn4.24:8.6: Evameva kho, bhante, yāni kānici visūkāyikāni visevitāni vipphanditāni, sabbāni tāni bhagavatā sañchinnāni sambhaggāni sampalibhaggāni.
root_text.sn4.24:8.7: Abhabbo dānāhaṁ, bhante, puna bhagavantaṁ upasaṅkamituṁ yadidaṁ otārāpekkho”ti.
root_text.sn4.24:8.8: Atha kho māro pāpimā bhagavato santike imā nibbejanīyā gāthāyo abhāsi:

translation_text.sn4.24:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying in Uruvelā at the goatherd’s banyan tree on the bank of the Nerañjarā River.
translation_text.sn4.24:1.3: Now at that time Māra the Wicked had been pursuing the Buddha for seven years hoping to find a vulnerability without success.
translation_text.sn4.24:1.4: Then Māra the Wicked went up to the Buddha and addressed him in verse:
translation_text.sn4.24:2.1: “Are you swamped by sorrow <j>that you meditate in the forest?
translation_text.sn4.24:2.2: Have you lost a fortune, or do you long for one?
translation_text.sn4.24:2.3: Or perhaps you’ve committed <j>some crime in the village?
translation_text.sn4.24:2.4: Why don’t you get too close to people?
translation_text.sn4.24:2.5: And why does no-one get close to you?”
translation_text.sn4.24:3.1: “I’ve dug out the root of sorrow completely.
translation_text.sn4.24:3.2: I practice absorption free of guilt or sorrow.
translation_text.sn4.24:3.3: I’ve cut off all greed and muttered prayer for future lives.
translation_text.sn4.24:3.4: Undefiled, I practice absorption, <j>O kinsman of the negligent!”
translation_text.sn4.24:5.1: “The things they speak of aren’t mine;
translation_text.sn4.24:5.2: I’m not someone who speaks like that.
translation_text.sn4.24:5.4: you won’t even see the path I take.”
translation_text.sn4.24:6.2: that’s safe, and leads to freedom from death,
translation_text.sn4.24:6.3: go and walk that path alone—
translation_text.sn4.24:6.4: why teach it to anyone else?”
translation_text.sn4.24:7.2: ask what’s beyond the domain of Death.
translation_text.sn4.24:7.3: When I’m asked, I explain to them
translation_text.sn4.24:8.1: “Sir, suppose there was a lotus pond not far from a town or village,
translation_text.sn4.24:8.3: Then several boys or girls would leave the town or village and go to the pond, where they’d pull out the crab and put it on solid ground.
translation_text.sn4.24:8.4: Whenever that crab extended a claw, those boys or girls would snap, crack, and break it off with a stick or a stone.
translation_text.sn4.24:8.5: And when that crab’s claws had all been snapped, cracked, and broken off it wouldn’t be able to return down into that lotus pond.
translation_text.sn4.24:8.6: In the same way, sir, the Buddha has snapped, cracked, and broken off all my twists, ducks, and dodges.
translation_text.sn4.24:8.7: Now I’m not able to approach the Buddha again in hopes of finding a vulnerability.”
translation_text.sn4.24:8.8: Then Māra the Wicked recited these verses of disappointment in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn4.24:9.2: that looked like a lump of fat.
translation_text.sn4.24:9.3: ‘Perhaps I’ll find something tender,’ it thought,
translation_text.sn4.24:10.3: Like the crow that pecked the stone,

variant_text.sn4.24:8.6: bhante, yāni kānici visūkāyikāni → yāni visukāyikāni (bj, mr); yāni kānici visukāyikāni (cck); yānikānici visūkāyitāni (sya1ed, sya2ed); yāni sukāyikāni (pts1ed) | vipphanditāni, sabbāni → kānici kānici sabbāni (bj, pts1ed, pts2ed, mr)

reference_text.sn4.24:8.1: dr15.170, ms12S1_907, ndp12.123, pts-vp-pli1ed1.124, pts-vp-pli2ed1.272, sya15.181
